source_jobs.title: Software Engineer II, Data Products
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

We're looking for a frontend-leaning Software Engineer II who loves building intuitive, data-rich user experiences and wants their work to matter. At Turquoise Health, we're making healthcare pricing transparent and understandable, and the interfaces you build will be how hospitals, insurers, and everyday people actually make sense of that data.

You’ll be a key member of a small, highly skilled engineering team, collaborating closely with the product team to shape the user experience from the component level up to the product decisions that influence them. While this is a full stack role and you'll touch backend systems, your primary impact will be in crafting the UI layer: turning complex healthcare pricing data into clean, interactive, and performant web experiences.

We're a fast moving startup, so you'll move fast too, taking on varied problems, shipping real features, and collaborating directly with the people who use what you build.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Development of Django-based websites using Python, Postgresql, etc.

 - Build frontend data visualization components that enable users to explore and interpret complex healthcare pricing data

 - Write effective, scalable code.

 - Test and debug programs

 - Improve functionality of existing systems

 - Assess and prioritize feature requests

 - Coordinate with other team members to understand user requirements and provide technical solutions


WHAT YOU'LL BRING TO THE ROLE:

 - 3+ years work experience in Python, or strong experience in another programming language and some exposure to Python

 - Proficiency in front-end technologies such as React, TypeScript and Vue.js

 - Expertise in at least one popular Python web framework (preferably Django) or a similar popular web framework in another language (like Ruby or Rails)

 - Good understanding of SQL and how to efficiently use databases to solve problems

 - Ability to work effectively in a remote environment

 - Good problem-solving skills

 - Good communication skills

 - Desire to learn new technologies

 - BS in Computer Science, Engineering or equivalent experience/knowledge. We are happy to work with strong candidates with non-traditional educational backgrounds


BONUS:

 - Experience working on open-source projects

 - Experience using with git and GitHub

 - Strong writing skills


BENEFITS:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend




ABOUT TURQUOISE HEALTH

Turquoise Health is a Series C price transparency platform for finance leaders across healthcare. Backed by a16z, Oak HC/FT, Adams Street, Yosemite, Bessemer Venture Partners, and others, we power price transparency for 300+ enterprise organizations and are building the infrastructure for a more open, efficient healthcare marketplace. We're a remote-first, US-based team that values transparency, empathy, inclusivity, creativity, and ownership.

We operate on US business hours and work with clients entirely based in the US. For this role, we are seeking US-based candidates.

We strongly encourage BIPOC, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA+ folks to apply for any open roles of interest. Healthcare affects all people differently, but it significantly affects those in underserved communities. With a robust, diverse team, we are stronger and better equipped to change the future of healthcare for all.




DISABILITY ACCOMMODATION EMAIL

Turquoise is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to app

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager/Principal, Provider RCM Business Development & Strategy
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role in the United States.

Are you a results-oriented, innovative and forward-thinking Business Development & Strategic Leader with strong expertise and passion for Revenue Cycle Management BD and Strategic Solution development? Here at Turquoise Health, we're seeking a dynamic Senior Manager/Principal to drive Provider RCM solution growth, develop novel capabilities, and work cross-functionally with customers and internal teams to shape the future of our RCM solutions.

This is a high-impact, high-visibility role supporting senior leadership as a thought partner in shaping how Turquoise serves our most valuable hospital and health system customers. You'll sit at the intersection of healthcare data and customer solution strategy, pulling and interpreting price transparency and reimbursement data, reasoning across the RCM landscape, and translating insights into board-ready narratives that move strategic pricing, payer contracting, and innovative tech-enabled solutions. You should be comfortable sitting at the intersection of business development and solution delivery. You should be someone with strong opinions, held loosely, who is equally comfortable in a SQL editor as you are in a commercial leader's office.

We strongly encourage BIPOC, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA+ folks to apply for any open roles of interest. Building a truly diverse team is a challenge that we do not shy away from. Healthcare affects all people differently, but it significantly affects those in underserved communities. With a robust, diverse team, we are stronger and better equipped to change the future of healthcare for all.

Responsibilities

As a Senior Manager/Principal in our Provider Vertical, you'll manage

Growth & Business Development

 - Partner with Sales and Customer Success to identify, scope, and win new engagements, shaping RFPs, proposals, statements of work, and value based pricing that reflect the value delivered.

 - Convert one-off projects into durable, multi-year partnerships across people, process, and technology by spotting adjacent opportunities across new and within existing accounts.

 - Act as a thought partner to senior leadership on how Turquoise packages, prices, and positions its solution based offerings.

Customer-Facing Partnerships

 - Serve as a trusted advisor to senior leaders such as VPs of Revenue Cycle and Managed Care, CFOs, and their teams at hospitals and health systems, framing ambiguous problems and translating them into structured, data-driven engagements across strategic pricing, payer contracting, charge capture, CDI, and other initiatives supporting net revenue improvement.

 - Own engagements end to end: scoping, work planning, execution, and delivery, holding yourself accountable for measurable client outcomes

 - Lead with expert subject matter and analytical methodology for each engagement. Pull and analyze price transparency, contracts, claims, and reimbursement data directly, pressure-test findings, and synthesize them into executive-ready narratives that withstand scrutiny.

 - Present findings and insights to client executives and help influence decisions on pricing strategy, contracting strategy, payer negotiations

Cross-Functional Partnership

 - Partner with Customer Success to align engagement outcomes with the broader account relationship and deliver a seamless customer experience.

 - Work with Solutions Engineering to source, structure, and validate the novel datasets that power customer work.

 - Coordinate with Finance, Legal, and Revenue Operations on contracting, engagement economics, and compliance.

 - Feed structured insight back to Product, surfacing recurring client needs, defining requirements for productization, and helping reduce cost-to-serve and improve scalability.

Requirements

What you bring to the Senior Manager/Principal role:

 - Bachelor's degree from a top tier institution required; MBA/MPH strongly preferred.

 - 8+ years in RCM-foc

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Science Engineer I, Spines
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

We’re looking for a driven, entrepreneurial Senior Data Science Engineer to join our team and help eliminate the financial complexity of healthcare. We value creative, outside-the-box thinkers that back their ideas with technical knowledge and action. In this role, you will join our Spines team, the core group responsible for building, maintaining, and understanding the backbone of our data infrastructure.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Work cross-functionally with Product and subject matter experts to conceptualize, prototype, and build data solutions

 - Build, unify, augment multi-source healthcare datasets into high-quality, Analytics-ready data models that power internal and external products

 - Build and maintain data engineering systems that support a wide range of downstream processes and products

 - Contribute to building, maintaining, and testing data pipelines

 - Draft internal and external technical documentation

 - Seek and prioritize technical and product feedback from internal customers

 - Iterate quickly with an eye towards value


WHAT YOU’LL BRING TO THE ROLE

 - 4+ years of experience developing data models, pipelines, and end-to-end analytical solutions using Python and SQL

 - Ability to design data systems with scalability, performance, and cost efficiency in mind, particularly for compute- and data-intensive workloads

 - Strong research and analytical skills

 - Strong grasp of data architecture and engineering practices, including ETL/ELT workflows, orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, dbt), and cloud-based storage (e.g., S3, Redshift)

 - Entrepreneurial mindset: you prioritize tasks with an eye for evolving business needs

 - Comfortable working remotely in a collaborative, technical team

 - Bachelor’s degree, or equivalent experience/knowledge. We are happy to work with strong candidates with non-traditional educational backgrounds


BONUS

 - Revenue cycle, healthcare payments and/or medical billing experience

 - Thoughtful use of AI coding agents and LLMs in development workflows

 - Knowledge of healthcare entities, how they work together and how they are structured

 - Strong experience in a research intensive field


BENEFITS

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid bi-annual in-person company gatherings

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend




ABOUT TURQUOISE HEALTH

Turquoise Health is a Series C price transparency platform for finance leaders across healthcare. Backed by a16z, Oak HC/FT, Adams Street, Yosemite, Bessemer Venture Partners, and others, we power price transparency for 300+ enterprise organizations and are building the infrastructure for a more open, efficient healthcare marketplace. We're a remote-first, US-based team that values transparency, empathy, inclusivity, creativity, and ownership.

We operate on US business hours and work with clients entirely based in the US. For this role, we are seeking US-based candidates.

We strongly encourage BIPOC, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA+ folks to apply for any open roles of interest. Healthcare affects all people differently, but it significantly affects those in underserved communities. With a robust, diverse team, we are stronger and better equipped to change the future of healthcare for all.

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer, Payer/Provider
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role in the United States.

Are you ready to join an exciting health tech startup revolutionizing the healthcare industry? Turquoise is looking for a talented Solutions Engineer with solid technical skills, a deep understanding of payer-provider negotiations, and healthcare contract pricing skills who is passionate about customer interaction, data-driven solutions, sales engineering, analytics, and products that spark joy for users.

As a Solutions Engineer, you will be a trusted advisor to payers and providers, guiding them through the intricate world of healthcare pricing data. Your expertise in our price transparency data warehouse will empower customers to drive negotiations and shape patient pricing strategies. You'll have the opportunity to create data-driven solutions, expand product adoption, and actively contribute to our data product roadmap.

We encourage you to bring your innovative, analytical, and resourceful mindset in this role. You'll have the chance to solve unique problems, manage multiple projects efficiently, and build strong customer relationships. We value collaboration and believe that success is measured by the active usage of our products post-sales.


Responsibilities

As a Solutions Engineer, you'll…

 - Be the go-to expert, partnering with customers to understand their data and reporting requirements, and effectively translating them to our engineering team

 - Educate customers through various channels such as email, blog posts, and webinars, ensuring they have the knowledge they need to succeed

 - Provide comprehensive analytics support, empowering customers with self-service tools and resources to access data

 - Tackle critical business and infrastructure issues through ad hoc analysis, insightful requests, and data extraction

 - Guide customers in migrating our data to their platforms and tools, adapting to diverse local environments

 - Collaborate closely with the product team, ensuring customer feedback drives our product development

 - Work hand in hand with our data engineering team, designing robust data structures and reliable data pipelines

 - Seek and prioritize technical feedback by engaging with customers

Requirements

What you bring to the Solutions Engineer role:

 - 3+ years of years of experience in healthcare data, such as revenue cycle/claims, patient access, quality data, or interoperability

 - 3+ years of experience working in payer-provider managed care contracting

 - Your analytical, problem-solving, mathematical, and creative thinking skills will be instrumental in driving impactful solutions

 - Excellent organizational and communication skills to work well both with internal stakeholders while also having the ability to create strong customer relationships

 - A drive to put out consistent high quality/accurate work at a fast pace

 - Communication and collaboration skills across seniority levels and functions.

 - Your excellent written communication and interpersonal skills will shine as you work with customers to achieve their goals

 - Your expertise in data modeling, large-scale data processing, and Excel will shape our data solutions

 - Your experience with visualization tools (Looker, Tableau) will enhance the customer experience

 - Your analytical and creative thinking will drive impactful solutions across complex business problems

Benefits:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend




ABOUT TURQUOISE HEALTH

source_jobs.title: Associate General Counsel
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

We’re eliminating the financial complexity of healthcare – and we need a lawyer who also thinks like a business person. This is not a ticket-queue job. It’s a seat at the table.

More specifically: we’re looking for a transactional attorney with a solid cross-functional foundation who’s ready to be a builder. If you’ve spent time at a firm or a larger in-house team and found yourself wanting more context, more ownership, and a front-row seat to how the business actually works – this is that role.

You’ll be the second attorney in Turquoise’s history, joining a GC who built the legal function from the ground up shortly after the company’s founding. The infrastructure is in place, and now we need someone to help scale it.

As Associate General Counsel, you’ll be an embedded partner across the company – Sales, Customer Success, Product, Engineering, Marketing, People Operations, and more – helping us move fast, close deals, and figure out the right answer on hard questions. You’ll also own the contracting function: drafting, negotiating, and building the infrastructure that lets Legal scale without becoming a bottleneck.

You’re the right fit if your instinct when a deal gets complicated is to find a path forward – not list the reasons it might be risky. You’re genuinely curious about the healthcare and health tech space, excited to go deep on a new regulatory question, and adaptable enough to thrive in an environment where the problems keep evolving. You don’t need a playbook for every situation – you’re ready to write one.

What you’ll be doing

 - Be the first point of contact for legal questions across the company – Sales, Customer Success, Product, Engineering, Marketing, People Operations, and more – triaging and resolving what you can, and collaborating with the GC on what needs to go further.

 - Own the contracting function end to end – drafting and negotiating the full range of agreements across customer, vendor, and operational matters, including MSAs, BAAs, SOWs, NDAs, ICAs, and Order Forms.

 - Build and own the infrastructure that keeps Legal running – the systems, processes, and programs that let the company move fast and stay compliant as it grows — and continuously look for ways to make them smarter, whether through AI tooling, better platforms, or cleaner workflows.

 - Interface with outside counsel on specialized matters – providing context, managing workflow, and implementing guidance back into the business.

 - Stay on top of the legal and regulatory landscape that shapes our business – healthcare data, price transparency, AI governance, privacy, and adjacent areas – and translate what’s changing into clear, actionable guidance for the team, including supporting government affairs and policy engagement as TQ’s visibility in the market grows.

 - Help build Legal’s presence and credibility across the company – through clear, proactive communication, internal guides and playbooks, and the kind of education that helps teammates move faster and make better decisions.

 - Support board governance and corporate policy work – maintaining records, preparing consents and minutes, and assisting with board materials as the company scales.

What you’ll bring to the role

 - JD from an accredited law school and active bar membership in at least one US state.

 - 4-7 years of transactional legal experience, with a meaningful focus on commercial contracts – at a firm, in-house, or both.

 - Strong working knowledge of SaaS and data licensing; experience negotiating with enterprise buyers is a plus.

 - Comfort with healthcare-adjacent regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, No Surprises Act, CMS price transparency) – or a genuine eagerness to learn them. We care more about how fast you pick things up than whether you already know every acronym.

 - You give guidance that actually helps people make decisions – not advice that hedges every direction and leaves the other person no

source_jobs.title: Content Producer, Executive Office
source_jobs.description:
This is a hybrid role based in San Diego, CA.




WHY WE'RE HERE

Americans have never spent more on healthcare, and we've never trusted the system less. The problem isn't any one insurer, hospital, or pharmaceutical company; it's a system so complicated that nobody can see how money moves through it.

Turquoise is on a mission to restore trust in the U.S. healthcare system by eliminating its financial complexity — for providers, payers, and patients most of all.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a creative storyteller and up-and-coming content creator who can take complex, dry concepts and turn them into content people actually want to consume. Think bite-sized documentaries for a general audience that explain how hospital pricing works, why your Explanation of Benefits (EOB) makes no sense, or what an insurance contract actually does. You'll work closely with our CEO and marketing team to produce multimedia content (video, audio/podcasts, social) that builds Turquoise's brand and educates the market.

This is a hybrid role based in San Diego, CA, reporting to the VP of Marketing with a strong dotted line to the CEO.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Make complex healthcare topics easier to understand and way less boring. Take dense, nuanced ideas and turn them into multimedia assets (video, audio, static) that feel human, simple, and worth watching. You do not need to be a healthcare expert to apply. We will invest in getting you up to speed!

 - Own end-to-end production. Plan the shoot, set up cameras, light the scene, capture clean audio, direct the subject, and make sure the footage is usable in edit. You will work with CEOs, customers, and teammates and help them feel natural and confident on camera.

 - Edit for attention and clarity. Create hooks, tighten narratives, cut for pace, add captions, select music, incorporate graphics or motion, and adapt edits for different formats and platforms.

 - Find stories across the company. Work with marketing, brand, product, sales, customer success, and leadership to uncover moments worth sharing.

 - Bring creative ideas to the table. Pitch new formats, suggest stronger openings, spot standalone clips, and help us experiment with what works across social and video.

 - Use AI and creative tools to move faster. Experiment with tools that help with scripting, editing, motion, visuals, repurposing, and production workflows.

 - Manage multiple projects at once. Keep projects organized, communicate clearly, meet deadlines, and stay flexible as priorities shift.
   


WHO YOU ARE

The job is storytelling first, production second.

 - Excellent at distilling complex topics. You’re intrinsically drawn to complex topics and you know how to make them legible to a non-expert. You find the analogy, cut the jargon, and make people feel smart for watching. (Think The Big Short.)

 - A natural-born explainer, with enough technical chops to produce engaging multimedia assets. You understand that a compelling story is a function of both content and form. Your instinct is both editorial (you know where the story should go) and technical (you know how to bring that story to life on video, audio, etc.)

 - Comfortable behind and in front of a camera. You can shoot, edit, and ship. The bar isn't Hollywood; it's "sounds good, looks intentional, holds attention." If you've made content for yourself or others, that counts.

 - San Diego-based (or ready to be). This role is in-person at our San Diego office, where you’ll spend one-on-one time mindmelding and riffing ideas with our founder/CEO.

Bonus: Sketch comedy, improv, or education background.




HOW TO APPLY

With your application, you’ll be asked to submit a 2–3 minute video explaining a complex financial topic to a general audience. You can be in the video or voice it over. Make it clear, engaging, and worth watching. Our CEO (a former video editor himself!) will review every submission that follows the rules.

Entirely AI-generated videos will be depri

source_jobs.title: Senior Enterprise Account Executive, Payer
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.


As part of our Enterprise Growth team at Turquoise Health, you will be the face of the company and directly responsible for sales and expansion in the segment. You will own the full sales cycle – building relationships with executive stakeholders, designing solutions, managing negotiations, and finalizing contracts with the largest companies in healthcare.

We need a hardworking, curious, entrepreneurial self-starter looking to change how we pay for healthcare and ready to bring a new product to market.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Identify new sales enterprise opportunities and develop them into high-value opportunities to fuel our growth at Turquoise Health

 - Build relationships and establish communications at the highest executive levels to understand their needs and priorities and to speed up and simplify the deal process

 - Own the closing process, including negotiations and procurement activities

 - Collaborate and share customer feedback with Product to identify new offerings to continuously increase the value of Turquoise Health

 - Diligently update Salesforce, our CRM, so we can learn more about our business and customers


WHAT YOU'LL BRING TO THE ROLE:

 - 5+ years of sales experience (in a closing role) at a technology or management consulting company with consistent attainment or out-performance of sales goals

 - Direct experience selling to health plans/payers, OR selling contract management/price transparency solutions. 

 - Successful track record of developing high-profile, long-term business relationships with executive stakeholders

 - Domain expertise of the healthcare system in the United States and a curiosity to keep learning the intricacies and changes within the industry 

 - Ability to make thoughtful, actionable recommendations and quickly build consensus with senior-level internal and external stakeholders

 - Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience/knowledge. We are happy to work with strong candidates with non-traditional educational backgrounds


BENEFITS:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend




ABOUT TURQUOISE HEALTH

Turquoise Health is a Series C price transparency platform for finance leaders across healthcare. Backed by a16z, Oak HC/FT, Adams Street, Yosemite, Bessemer Venture Partners, and others, we power price transparency for 300+ enterprise organizations and are building the infrastructure for a more open, efficient healthcare marketplace. We're a remote-first, US-based team that values transparency, empathy, inclusivity, creativity, and ownership.

We operate on US business hours and work with clients entirely based in the US. For this role, we are seeking US-based candidates.

We strongly encourage BIPOC, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA+ folks to apply for any open roles of interest. Healthcare affects all people differently, but it significantly affects those in underserved communities. With a robust, diverse team, we are stronger and better equipped to change the future of healthcare for all.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer II, AI
source_jobs.description:
This is a full remote role within the United States. Preference is for this person to be based in Chicago.

We are looking for a Software Engineer to join our AI team and help eliminate the financial complexity of healthcare. This role is for engineers who are motivated by highly visible, high-impact work and excited to build and operate AI-enabled systems. You’ll work closely with senior engineers, learning firsthand how they build and ship in a startup environment. This is a role to grow quickly, contributing to AI-enabled features and workflows.

Responsibilities

 - Contribute to internal services relied on by other engineers

Here’s what you bring to the role:

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Data
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

As a Product Manager specializing in Data, you will help build out and chart the course for Turquoise Health’s data products. You will come into a maturing product with significant existing traction, and help build out data features with immediate customer impact.

We’re looking for a business-minded Product Manager with the ability to prioritize in situations where time and resources are scarce, and who excels at pitching a business case for investing time and attention to a particular product focus. You will get to work with industry and customer feedback to prioritize features for our expanding data products whose purpose is to bring transparency and structure to the healthcare pricing landscape across providers, payers, employers, life science manufacturers, and beyond.

A qualified candidate should bring significant healthcare data expertise to the role, but is also open and curious to learn about the nuances of Turquoise’s business and that of our customers. You develop strong opinions, loosely held; it’s a startup after all, and things change quickly!

Most importantly, you are mission-driven and (like the rest of us) are dead set on simplifying pricing and payments in healthcare.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Define functional requirements for new data features and products in partnership with engineering, product, and go-to-market teams

 - Develop and implement data product strategies consistent with the company's vision

 - Work with senior management to create product plans and roadmaps

 - Collaborate closely with engineering to estimate effort, make feature trade-offs, and advocate for more resources when needed

 - Enable sales, marketing, and customer success teams to go to market and support data products

 - Ensure products and releases are launched correctly and on schedule

 - Collect and analyze feedback from customers, stakeholders, and other teams to shape requirements, features, and end products

 - Make creative recommendations to expand the product base and vision


WHAT YOU'LL BRING TO THE ROLE:

 - 3+ years of experience working closely with healthcare data either within or tangential to revenue cycle or managed care

 - Deep experience working with big, messy data sets, data processing pipelines, and data warehouses (far beyond a simple SQL DB)

 - Fluency in SQL, with python proficiency a plus. Experience working with EDI/x12 is also a plus

 - Intellectually curious and opinionated about the market—able to form and defend thoughtful perspectives on customer needs, competitive dynamics, and industry trends.

 - Forward-looking and creative in experimenting with AI tools to accelerate product discovery, analysis, and execution

 - Demonstrated experience with viability experiments and testing product hypotheses on limited resources

 - Ability to communicate complex technical product concepts to a non-technical audience

 - Comfort articulating, researching, and estimating ROI for product decisions

 - Technical fluency in communicating with designers and engineers, with a solid intuition for technical effort requirements

 - Drive to work in a fast-paced, amorphous early-stage environment where decisions are often made with imperfect information

 - Communication and collaboration skills across seniority levels and functions


BENEFITS:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend

source_jobs.title: Data Science Engineer, Analytics
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

We’re looking for a driven, entrepreneurial Data Science Engineer to join our team and help eliminate the financial complexity of healthcare. You'll join our Data Science Engineering team supporting analytics, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and business stakeholders to help Turquoise grow and scale our impact by making better decisions with data. From defining product metrics and measuring customer engagement to enabling self-service analytics and evaluating business initiatives, you'll build the datasets, pipelines, dashboards, and analyses that transform data into actionable insights across the company.



Responsibilities

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, and business stakeholders to define metrics, measure outcomes, and evaluate impact

 - Build and maintain data pipelines, data models, dashboards, and analytical infrastructure that support product, operational, and strategic decision-making

 - Conduct analyses to understand product adoption, customer engagement, business performance, and operational efficiency

 - Contribute to the development of analytics best practices, shared datasets, and company-wide metrics

 - Seek and act on feedback from internal stakeholders; iterate quickly with an eye toward value


What you’ll bring to the role

 - Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience. Non-traditional backgrounds welcome

 - 2+ years developing data models, pipelines, and end-to-end analytical solutions in Python and SQL. Comfortable with OOP and functional patterns, code organization beyond scripts, and debugging workflows

 - Experience with dataframe libraries (pandas, polars)

 - Experience with ETL/ELT workflows and orchestration (Airflow, dbt)

 - Comfort with cloud services (AWS S3, EC2, RDS)

 - Ability to design data systems with scalability, performance, and cost efficiency in mind, particularly for compute- and data-intensive workloads

 - Entrepreneurial mindset: you prioritize tasks with an eye for evolving business needs

 - Comfortable working remotely in a collaborative, technical team



Bonus points for experience with

 - Experience with b2b healthcare product analytics

 - Experience applying quantitative methods (regression, causal inference) to product or business questions

 - Thoughtful use of AI coding agents and LLMs in development workflows



Benefits

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid bi-annual in-person company gatherings

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend




ABOUT TURQUOISE HEALTH

Turquoise Health is a Series C price transparency platform for finance leaders across healthcare. Backed by a16z, Oak HC/FT, Adams Street, Yosemite, Bessemer Venture Partners, and others, we power price transparency for 300+ enterprise organizations and are building the infrastructure for a more open, efficient healthcare marketplace. We're a remote-first, US-based team that values transparency, empathy, inclusivity, creativity, and ownership.

We operate on US business hours and work with clients entirely based in the US. For this role, we are seeking US-based candidates.

We strongly encourage BIPOC, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA+ folks to apply for any open roles of interest. Healthcare affects all people differently, but it significantly affects those in underserved communities. With a robust, diverse team, we are stronger and better equipped to change the future of healthcare for all.




DISABILITY ACCOMMODATION EMAIL

Turquoise is committed to providing reasonable accomm

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Data Products
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

The Data Products organization at Turquoise is transforming how healthcare prices are understood, helping to flatten healthcare price inflation across the US, and building the foundational layer for direct to consumer healthcare transactions in the future.

We are seeking a skilled and proven Engineering Manager to lead one of our Data Product Engineering teams. You will play a critical role in guiding the technical direction of our user-facing products, fostering collaboration between engineering, product, and design, and driving a culture of excellence. We are looking for a leader who is passionate about building high-quality products and empowering their team to deliver exceptional user experiences.




RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Team Leadership: Lead, mentor, and grow a team of product engineers, responsible for building data analysis products on top of Turquoise’s best-in-class health service pricing data.

 - Product Delivery: Own the technical execution and delivery of key product features, ensuring high quality, performance, and scalability.

 - Technical Direction: Provide technical guidance and oversight, specifically in modern frontend technologies (VueJS), ensuring best practices in coding, architecture, and design are followed.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with Product Managers, Designers, and other Engineers to translate requirements into technical solutions and ensure alignment with business goals. Foster a high-performance and inclusive culture.

 - Quality & Excellence: Champion a culture of high quality, conducting code reviews and ensuring the product meets rigorous standards for user experience and reliability.


WHAT YOU'LL BRING TO THE ROLE:

 - 5+ years experience in product engineering, or a related role, with 2+ years of experience managing a team of engineers.

 - Proven expertise shipping reliable products with high quality user experiences.

 - A track record of building inclusive teams, with process decisions informed by bottom-up insight from engineers.

 - Proven expertise managing and evolving incident response practices and managing an on-call schedule.

 - Experience working with cloud infrastructure (AWS) and platform services such as Heroku.

 - Proficiency with monitoring and observability tools (DataDog).

 - Excellent leadership and interpersonal skills, with the ability to convey technical issues to non-technical audiences.

 - Strong collaboration skills and ability to work across functional teams.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience.


BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience working in SaaS or data-intensive environments.

 - Familiarity with compliance (HIPAA) and security frameworks (e.g., SOC 2, ISO-27001).

 - Familiarity and experience with Python, Django, and VueJS.

 - Experience in a remote-first environment.


BENEFITS:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Personalization
source_jobs.description:
This is fully remote role within the United States.

We are seeking a systems-minded, highly collaborative Senior Product Manager to architect and build the foundational experience that powers the entire Turquoise platform. This product owner will own the core connective tissue, including onboarding, preferences, notifications, and reporting, ensuring that every vertical and feature scales seamlessly and feels like a single, coherent user experience.

With support from Engineering, Design, Data, and GTM teams, your ability to untangle deep technical complexity and design durable primitives will directly enable the company to ship faster and scale our mission of simplifying healthcare pricing.



Why this Role Matters

The Personalization team builds the foundational building blocks that the rest of Turquoise relies on. By designing elegant, horizontal surfaces and platform infrastructure, this role directly multiplies the impact of every other product team and ensures a unified, intuitive journey for our customers.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the platform’s core surfaces end-to-end, including onboarding, permissions, preferences, and notifications from strategy through iteration.

 - Champion the end-to-end user experience, map real user flows and journeys, and shape intuitive, cohesive interactions across every surface.

 - Think in systems to design primitives and patterns that compose cleanly across products, anticipating second-order effects and prioritizing long-term foundations.

 - Turn messy, ambiguous horizontal challenges into concrete, elegant requirements and shipped software.

 - Establish direction with data, defining clear success metrics and leveraging analytics to guide future iteration.

 - Leverage the latest AI prototyping tools to pressure-test ideas and build rapid proofs-of-concept before committing engineering time.

 - Facilitate airtight coordination across engineering, design, data, and GTM functions to ship cohesive products.


WHAT YOU'LL BRING TO THE ROLE

 - 5+ years of Product Management experience, with a proven track record of shipping and iterating on shared components of a user-facing platform in a B2B SaaS environment.

 - Deep platform expertise, specifically managing connective experience, such as onboarding, lifecycle notifications, or permissions.

 - A design-thinking instinct - you naturally empathize with users, think in flows and journeys, and sweat the quality of the end-to-end experience, not just the individual feature.

 - Exceptional systems thinking, with a natural ability to reason about dependencies, edge cases, data models, and architectural constraints alongside engineers.

 - An AI-native workflow, comfortable utilizing modern AI tools to quickly turn product concepts into working proofs-of-concept.

 - A metrics-driven framework, using customer interviews and product analytics to validate success and make fast, informed decisions.

 - High EQ and comfort with ambiguity, combining strong communication skills with the grit and judgment to drive progress amidst imperfect information.

 - A strong mission alignment and a genuine passion for reducing complexity to make healthcare better.


BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience with recommendation, relevance, or ranking systems

 - Experience with building internal tools your coworkers love

 - Experience in a regulated, data-heavy domain like fintech, health tech

 - Experience at a rapidly scaling, venture-backed startup where things change quickly


BENEFITS:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous pai

source_jobs.title: Platform Operations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role within the United States.

Turquoise Health is building its Platform Engineering function from the ground up and this is your chance to shape it. As our first Platform Operations Engineer, you'll help lay the foundation for how we build, deploy, and operate infrastructure that supports real-world healthcare outcomes.

This is a high-ownership role on a new team. You'll help establish the practices, tooling, and standards that the rest of engineering will rely on, from observability and reliability to deployment workflows and scalability. If you're energized by greenfield problems, comfortable navigating ambiguity, and motivated to automate and simplify before being asked, we'd love to build something great together.


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Support the Platform Infrastructure - Help manage and scale our container environment on Amazon EKS, implement GitOps workflows using ArgoCD, and maintain CI/CD pipelines through GitHub Actions to ensure that deployments are fast, consistent, and automated

 - Build for Reliability - Define and track SLIs and SLOs, lead incident response including on-call rotations, root cause analysis, and post-mortems, and contribute to disaster recovery planning to keep our systems highly available

 - Drive Observability - Design and maintain our monitoring and logging stack using Datadog, Sentry, and CloudWatch — giving engineering teams clear visibility into system health and performance before problems reach users

 - Shape the Platform's Future - Collaborate on architectural decisions, build internal tooling and self-service workflows that make the platform easier to operate, and contribute meaningfully to how we scale and evolve our infrastructure


WHAT YOU’LL BRING:

 - 3+ years in SRE, DevOps, or Cloud Infrastructure

 - Confident working with core AWS services (VPC, IAM, EKS, RDS) and a strong understanding of cloud networking and security best practices

 - Expert in using Infrastructure as code with Terraform, CloudFormation, or Crossplane

 - Proficient with GitHub and GitHub Actions as a core component of your CI/CD and automation pipelines- not just for source control

 - Experienced with running Kubernetes clusters in production and managing application deployments through GitOps workflows (ArgoCD/Flux) and Helm Charts

 - Proficient with observability tooling such as Datadog, Sentry, CloudWatch, Grafana to include building alerts, dashboards, and log pipelines

 - Experience writing solid Python scripts to glue systems together, automate infrastructure tasks, or handle custom workflows

 - Comfortable working independently in a remote setup, asking questions when needed, and keeping momentum without being micromanaged

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience. Happy to work with strong candidates with non-traditional educational backgrounds


NICE TO HAVES:

 - Certifications: AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform or Python


BENEFITS:

 - Competitive pay with equity options

 - Stellar health care plan options (Medical, Dental & Vision), with FSA, DCFSA, & HSA options

 - Company-sponsored disability & life insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 401(k) + 4% Matching

 - Fully remote work + flexible working hours

 - $750 work-from-home setup budget

 - Paid biannual in-person company summits

 - Quarterly $150 co-hanging stipend to meet up with coworkers

 - Monthly $100 health and wellness benefit

 - Generous paid family leave

 - Annual $1,200 learning & development stipend




ABOUT TURQUOISE HEALTH

Turquoise Health is a Series C price transparency platform for finance leaders across healthcare. Backed by a16z, Oak HC/FT, Adams Street, Yosemite, Bessemer Venture Partners, and others, we power price transparency for 300+ enterprise organizations and are building the infrastructure for a more open, efficient healthcare marketplace. We're a remote-first, US-based team that values transparency, empathy, inclusivity, creativity, and owners
